rows.description: Soluta Varia.
rows.notes: Clear page title, underlined and centered at top. No amounts on this line.

rows.description: Gratuity to Jackson
rows.notes: Text and amount very clear. Pounds/shillings/pence aligned to the ruling lines and read as 20 0 0.

rows.description: File on Account pr Campfield
rows.notes: Amount 10 0 0 is clear. The first word looks like 'File' but could be 'Bill' (handwriting ambiguity). Chosen 'File' based on visible initial letter and loops.

rows.description: Mr Falconer for repair of Bushey Tenements
rows.notes: Amount 10 0 0 is clearly aligned. The tenant/place word is faint and could be 'Bushey' or similar; 'Falconer' and 'repair' are legible.

rows.description: Stringer for Receipt Stamps
rows.notes: Shillings and pence read clearly as 10s 6d. The pounds column is effectively zero (no pound figure); the first word looks like 'Stringer' (could be 'Stinger' or similar) but context 'for Receipt Stamps' is clear.

rows.description: Subscription to new Chapel in St. Mary Magdn
rows.notes: Description and amount (30 0 0) are clearly legible; 'Magdn' is the common abbreviation for Magdalen.

rows.description: E. Pile, late College Groom, 5/. per week
rows.notes: Description is clear (E. Pile etc.). Amount reads 13 pounds, 5 shillings, 0 pence with good alignment.

rows.description: Payment (crossed out) [text largely illegible]
rows.notes: Entire line is crossed through / cancelled on the page. The numerals 10 0 0 are visible but the descriptive text is heavily struck through and mostly illegible; included as an entry because amounts are present but strike-through reduces confidence.

rows.description: Receipt Stamps
rows.notes: Short description 'Receipt Stamps' is clear and amount 1 0 0 is well aligned and legible.

rows.description: Mr Hastings on acct of work at Campfield
rows.notes: Description and large amount (200 0 0) are clearly written and aligned to the columns.

rows.description: Poor of South Newington for Coal
rows.notes: Text 'Poor of South Newington for Coal' is legible; amount 5 0 0 clearly aligned.

rows.description: Repairing pumps &c at Sherton
rows.notes: Amount 11 4 0 is clear. Place name looks like 'Sherton' (could be 'Sherston'); phrase 'Repairing pumps &c' is legible.

rows.description: Spinkers for Lime
rows.notes: Amount 1 4 0 is readable. The first word is unclear — read as 'Spinkers' but could be a different occupational name; context 'for Lime' is clear.

rows.description: Mr Glubb, Dischard 2 2d Agency
rows.notes: Amount 4 4 0 is clear. The descriptive phrase contains abbreviations/uncertain words ('Dischard 2 2d Agency' is a best reading of the cramped script).

rows.description: Sandell, one days work at Rectory, Kidlington
rows.notes: Amount 2s 6d is clearly written; pounds column effectively zero. 'Sandell' and 'Rectory, Kidlington' read well.

rows.description: Provisional Friend - Mr Stapart to Xmas
rows.notes: Amount 12 10 0 is clear. The name is cramped and ambiguous (read as 'Mr Stapart' or 'Mr Shupart' or 'Mr Hyatt'); 'Provisional Friend' phrase is legible but the individual's surname is uncertain.

rows.description: Kidlington Chancel
rows.notes: Short description 'Kidlington Chancel' and amount 9 0 0 are clearly legible and aligned.

rows.description: Case for Packing Glass for Do
rows.notes: Amount reads 7s 6d clearly; pounds column blank (treated as 0). Description 'Case for Packing Glass for Do' (Do = ditto/Kidlington) is legible.

rows.description: Davies, mounting Almanack for Lord Petre
rows.notes: Amount 10 0 0 is clear. 'Davies, mounting Almanack for Lord Petre' matches the script; 'Almanack' reading chosen for the context.

rows.description: Property Tax - 1st half year
rows.notes: Amount 29 3 7 is clearly written and aligns with the columns. Description is legible.

rows.description: Gas Company
rows.notes: Short description 'Gas Company' is clear. Amount 4 3 10 is legible and within valid ranges.

rows.description: Subscription - (Church name faint)
rows.notes: Amount 5 0 0 is clear. The word after 'Subscription -' (church name) is faint and somewhat smudged; included as 'Church name faint' to indicate uncertainty in the specific church name.

rows.description: Mr Cutterbuck for repairs of Cottages at L. Whittle
rows.notes: Amount 65 5 5 is clear. Surname 'Cutterbuck' and phrase 'repairs of Cottages' are legible; the place 'L. Whittle' is read from abbreviated script and may be slightly uncertain.

rows.description: Purchase Money of the Fee in reversion of the new buildings in Broad St.
rows.notes: Very large sum clearly written as 2100 0 0 and bracketed across several lines. The descriptive phrasing is legible and matches the legal purchase wording.

rows.description: Mr Hester the Town Clerk's bill in the matter
rows.notes: Amount 16 12 3 is clear. 'Mr Hester the Town Clerk's bill' is readable and contextually sensible.

rows.description: Two thirds of Mr Dives's bill for valuing the same
rows.notes: Amount 36 4 0 is legible. The name reads as 'Mr Dives' (could be 'Mr Drives' or similar); phrase 'Two thirds of ... bill for valuing the same' is inferred from handwriting and context.

rows.description: Loan to the Rector - 1/2 of third instalment for improvements at Stratfield
rows.notes: Amount 50 0 0 is clearly written. Descriptive phrase is legible but place-name 'Stratfield' (or similar) is read from abbreviated script and may be slightly uncertain.
Final total at bottom of page (no textual label present, only the summed figures). Numerals read clearly as 2625 12 11 and align to the ruling lines.
